meanings.definition: the act of causing something to move up and down (or back and forth) with quick movements

examples: the shaking of his fingers as he lit his pipe

meanings.definition: move or cause to move back and forth
examples: The chemist shook the flask vigorously

meanings.definition: move with or as if with a tremor
shake or vibrate rapidly and intensively
move back and forth or sideways
examples: She rocked back and forth on her feet
The bad news shook her hopes

meanings.definition: stir the feelings, emotions, or peace of
examples: the civil war shook the country
I couldn't shake the car that was following me

meanings.definition: bring to a specified condition by or as if by shaking
examples: He was shaken from his dreams
shake the salt out of the salt shaker

meanings.definition: shake (a body part) to communicate a greeting, feeling, or cognitive state
examples: She shook her finger at the naughty students
Don't shake your fist at me!
